block_03: Last week Non-Farm Payrolls were out, showing a colossal release of 850,000 new jobs in the US compared to 583,000 in May. However, the unemployment rate also showed an increase of ±0.1%, resulting from 5.9%. Please take special note of the USD pairs, as such mixed results might unpredictably affect them.
block_04: Among other news, The United Arab Emirates blocked a deal with OPEC+ at the last minute, causing the alliance to put off its decision on monthly production. Before the disagreement, OPEC+ appeared to agree to boost output by 400,000 barrels a day each month from August through December. And now, let’s move on to the latest reading materials.

block_16: Most Profitable Trade of the Week
block_42: According to Credit Suisse research published in 2010, Australia was the best-performing country in terms of share market’ results starting from 1900 and until 2009. During that period, the country posted the highest after-inflation returns of 7.5% yearly and the 2nd lowest volatility level compared to the nineteen markets that took part in the research.
